LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SON TRUNG LE'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITH TWENTY DAYS TO ANSWER. THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS ADEQUATELY PLED AND THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT SHOW THAT THE CLAIM IS NECESSARILY TIME-BARRED AS TO ALL RELIEF THAT IS SOUGHT. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF HENRY YIP'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT LE'S AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, TENTH AND TWELVE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE REMAINING AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ARE SUFFICIENTLY PLED AND THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THOSE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RONALD E. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-21-2016 Set For Trial On Aug-08-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Son Trung Le's demurrer to the complaint is overruled with twenty days to answer. The sole cause of action for breach of contract is adequately pled and the face of the complaint does not show that the claim is necessarily time-barred as to all relief that is sought. The prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported.
